content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted all three amendments. They each address real correctness/robustness defects that can cause false failures or mis-attributed reconciliation state without adding functional behavior.

## Votes

- `fr-10-c43dddd4`: Accept
  `existing_markers_seen` is only an idempotency signal, not evidence that this call made a mutation. Calling `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy` unconditionally for marker-only findings can fail due unrelated pending/foreign mutation state and break replay. Gating sync on actual posted comments or verified own pending mutations is the safer behavior.
- `fr-10-4816d9d7`: Accept
  `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` performs I/O (`proposed-beads.ndjson`) before classifying/thresholding proposals. This means no-op paths (`FixCurrentBead`, `InformationalOnly`, or below-threshold groups) can fail on unrelated file errors. Deferring file reads until at least one proposal group meets threshold preserves idempotent/no-op behavior.
- `fr-10-41a341a5`: Accept
  `reconcile_cli_terminal_bead_state` passes `project_id` where `task_id` should be passed. The CLI path thus mislabels task context in terminal reconciliation error/warning/metadata paths. Correcting the second argument restores proper task attribution and idempotent tracking consistency.
